kind: dense data-model fast path for Argmax/Argmin (different primitive than sort)
lever_summary: argmax/argmin scanned each element via sort_key+compare_sort_keys. Added dense f64 (as_f64_slice, total_cmp) and i64 (as_i64_slice, cmp) scans with the same strict-greater(max)/strict-less(min) first-occurrence update, bypassing the per-element Literal machinery.
method: same-bench A/B; baseline = stashed generic scan; normalized by eval/sort_calib_reduce_64k_i64 (ReduceSum, unaffected).

change.lever: extremum_along_axis: added a shared base_of closure + dense f64 (total_cmp) and i64 (cmp) scan branches before the generic else-branch. Added eval/argmax_64k_f64 bench + argmax_argmin_dense_matches_generic test.

isomorphism.type: BIT-IDENTICAL indices vs generic: same total_cmp(F64)/cmp(I64), strict update (first-occurrence ties), best init at index 0; same NaN/+-0/+-inf ordering

validation.name: argm_tests
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-lax --release argm

validation.name: clippy
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo clippy -p fj-lax --release --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings

note: Different primitive from the sort/topk radix vein. Dense vein now covers reductions + cumulative + argmax/argmin.
